text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Bekka.
zh: 我是贝卡。

text: Cycling is a popular activity in Britain.
zh: 骑自行车在英国是一项很受欢迎的活动。

text: Yes.
zh: 是的。

text: This year, sales of bicycles, or bikes as they're usually called, are up in Britain.
zh: 今年,英国自行车的销量有所上升。

text: Many people choose cycling because it's good for their health and the environment.
zh: 很多人选择骑车,因为这对健康和环境都有好处。

text: And electric bikes are making cycling accessible to more people, including older people.
zh: 电动自行车让更多人,包括老年人,都能骑上自行车。

text: In this episode, we'll visit two countries, one with a long history of cycling and another where it's just getting started.
zh: 在这一集里,我们将探访两个国家,一个有着悠久的骑行历史,另一个则刚刚起步。

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 像往常一样,我们会学习一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期的所有词汇。

text: But first, Neil, I have a question about a country that's world famous for its bikes – the Netherlands, also often referred to as Holland.
zh: 不过首先,尼尔,我有个问题,和一个以自行车闻名于世的国家有关——荷兰,也常被称为Holland。

text: The Netherlands cycle network spreads over 95 ,000 miles and there are more bikes in the country than its 18 million citizens.
zh: 荷兰的自行车道网络总长达9.5万英里,而这个国家自行车的数量甚至超过了其1800万的居民人数。

text: So, roughly speaking, how many bikes are there in the Netherlands?
zh: 那么,大致来说,荷兰有多少辆自行车呢?

text: Is it A. 20 million, B. 24 million or C. 34 million?
Well, I'm going to say C. 34 million.
zh: 嗯,我选C,3400万。

text: Ah, well, we'll reveal the correct answer later in the programme.
zh: 好,我们稍后会在节目中公布正确答案。

text: Staying in the Netherlands, let's hear from Meredith Glaeser, CEO of Amsterdam's Urban Cycling Institution.
zh: 继续聊荷兰的话题,我们来听听阿姆斯特丹城市骑行研究所首席执行官梅雷迪思·格拉泽的说法。

text: Here, Meredith tells BBC World Service programme The Enquiry more about Dutch people's love of cycling.
zh: 在这里,梅雷迪思向BBC世界服务节目《The Enquiry》讲述了更多荷兰人对骑行的热爱。

text: The bicycle is deeply ingrained into Dutch culture, but the rise of cycling started in the industrial era when bicycle manufacturing took off.
zh: 自行车深深植根于荷兰文化之中,而骑行的兴起始于工业时代,当时自行车制造业开始蓬勃发展。

text: off.
zh: 蓬勃发展。

text: And it went from a toy for rich people to an everyday tool for transportation.
zh: 自行车从富人的玩具变成了人人使用的日常交通工具。

text: And this was a really important milestone for cycling in the Netherlands.
zh: 这对荷兰的骑行发展来说,是一个非常重要的里程碑。

text: Cycling is deeply ingrained into Dutch culture.
zh: 骑行深深植根于荷兰文化之中。

text: Deeply ingrained are two words that go together commonly and it means something that's firmly established within a person or culture and unlikely to change.
zh: “深深植根”(deeply ingrained)是两个常搭配使用的词,意思是某种事物在一个人或一种文化中根深蒂固,不太可能改变。

text: During the 1890s, bike manufacturing took off in the Netherlands.
zh: 在19世纪90年代,荷兰的自行车制造业开始迅速发展。

text: Take -off is a phrasal verb meaning to suddenly become popular or successful.
zh: “take off”是一个短语动词,意思是突然变得流行或成功。

text: Certain brands became symbols of a new, comfortable style of bike.
zh: 某些品牌成为了一种新式舒适自行车的象征。

text: New factories made bikes cheaper to buy.
zh: 新工厂的出现让自行车的价格更加便宜。

text: This was a milestone for cycling in the Netherlands.
zh: 这是荷兰骑行史上的一个里程碑。

text: A milestone is an important event in someone's life or in the development of something.
zh: “里程碑”(milestone)指的是某人一生中或某事物发展过程中的重要事件。

text: Our second destination is better known for cars than bicycles – the United Arab Emirates, or UAE.
zh: 我们要探访的第二个国家,以汽车而非自行车闻名——阿拉伯联合酋长国,简称阿联酋。

text: is hoping cycling could be a greener way for visitors to explore tourist attractions in cities like Dubai and Abu Dhabi.
zh: 阿联酋希望骑行能成为游客游览迪拜和阿布扎比等城市景点的一种更环保方式。

text: Transport consultant Martin Tillman, who lives in the Gulf region, explained more to BBC World Service programme The Enquiry.
zh: 居住在海湾地区的交通顾问马丁·蒂尔曼向BBC世界服务节目《The Enquiry》作了进一步说明。

text: Martin Tillman, Transport Consultant, UAE World Service Programme competitive and they would love to be ranked very high in those global rankings.
zh: 马丁·蒂尔曼,阿联酋交通顾问,在BBC世界服务节目中提到,各城市之间竞争激烈,都希望在全球排名中名列前茅。

text: And cycling is seen as part of that quality of life solution.
zh: 而骑行被视为提升生活质量的一部分方案。

text: Gulf cities like Dubai want a reputation as places with a good quality of life.
zh: 像迪拜这样的海湾城市希望树立起高生活质量的形象。

text: The phrase quality of life refers to someone's level of enjoyment, comfort and health when living in a particular place.
zh: “生活质量”(quality of life)指一个人在特定地方生活时所享受到的愉悦感、舒适度和健康水平。

text: It's a measure of a person's overall wellbeing.
zh: 它是衡量一个人整体幸福感的一个标准。

text: being.
zh: 幸福感。

text: Gulf cities are also interested in livability, the degree to which somewhere is a pleasant place to live.
zh: 海湾城市同样关注“宜居性”(livability),即一个地方作为居住地的舒适程度。

text: Cycling is good for mental as well as physical health, and Dubai's town planners are hoping the 600 kilometres of cycle tracks already built will make the city more attractive to live in.
zh: 骑行不仅有益身体健康,也有助于心理健康,迪拜的城市规划者希望,已经建成的600公里自行车道能让这座城市变得更宜居。

text: Of course, there are big differences between the Netherlands and UAE.
zh: 当然,荷兰和阿联酋之间存在很大差异。

text: The temperature for for one, which in Dubai regularly hits 45 degrees Celsius.
zh: 其中之一就是气温,迪拜的气温常常高达45摄氏度。

text: Martin told BBC World Service's The Inquiry that Dubai is studying countries like the Netherlands, not to copy them, but to discover what works.
zh: 马丁告诉BBC世界服务节目《The Enquiry》,迪拜正在研究荷兰等国家的经验,并不是要照搬,而是要发掘其中的可行之道。

text: It's certainly not a copy and paste of what is good in Holland or Denmark, but the key is that we're looking for the principles.
zh: 这绝不是简单地把荷兰或丹麦的经验“复制粘贴”过来,关键在于我们要提炼其中的原则。

text: Cycling in UAE isn't a copy and paste of cycling in Holland.
zh: 阿联酋的骑行模式并不是荷兰模式的“复制粘贴”。

text: Martin uses the phrase a copy and paste to mean an exact copy of something which is moved and put somewhere else.
zh: 马丁用“copy and paste”(复制粘贴)这个短语,来指把某样东西原封不动地搬到另一个地方。

text: Dubai can't simply copy the Netherlands, and a range of measures, including water stations and shaded rest areas, have been created for cycling in the heat.
zh: 迪拜无法简单地照搬荷兰的做法,因此为了应对高温骑行,当地设置了包括补水站和遮阳休息区在内的一系列措施。

text: Cycling isn't the perfect solution for everyone, everywhere, but in some places it can be a popular and green alternative.
zh: 骑行并不是适用于所有地方、所有人的完美方案,但在某些地方,它可以成为一种受欢迎的绿色出行选择。

text: Yes, just look at the Netherlands, and that reminds me of your question, Becca.
zh: 是啊,看看荷兰就知道了,这也让我想起了你之前的问题,贝卡。

text: Ah yes, I asked you roughly how many bikes are there in the Netherlands?
zh: 哦对,我之前问你,荷兰大约有多少辆自行车?

text: I said 34 million.
zh: 我说的是3400万。

text: Was I right?
zh: 我说对了吗?

text: There are certainly a lot of bikes in the Netherlands.
zh: 荷兰的自行车数量确实非常多。

text: However, there are only, roughly, 24 million bikes.
zh: 不过,大致来说,只有约2400万辆。

text: Only?
zh: 只有?

text: That's still quite a lot!
zh: 那也已经很多了!

text: Right, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with deeply ingrained – a combination of words meaning well -established and unlikely to change.
zh: 好,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是“deeply ingrained”——一个词组,意思是根深蒂固、不易改变。

text: If something takes off, it suddenly becomes popular or successful.
zh: 如果某事物“take off”,就是说它突然变得流行或成功。

text: A milestone is an important event in the development of something or someone.
zh: “milestone”(里程碑)指某事或某人发展过程中的重要事件。

text: Quality of life describes a person's wellbeing in terms of their level of enjoyment, comfort and health.
zh: “quality of life”(生活质量)描述一个人在愉悦感、舒适度和健康方面的整体状况。

text: The noun liveability means the degree to which a place is pleasant to live in.
zh: 名词“livability”(宜居性)指一个地方作为居住地的舒适程度。

text: And finally, a copy and paste is an exact copy of something which is then moved and put somewhere else.
zh: 最后,“copy and paste”(复制粘贴)指把某样东西原封不动地搬到另一个地方。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you can find a transcript and a worksheet and a quiz all over on our website at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,别忘了你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到文字稿、练习题和小测验。

text: Goodbye for now.
zh: 我们下次再见。

text: Bye.
zh: 再见。
